mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad collapse in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized during high inflation periods signals severe capacity constraints that compress profit margins, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: High industrial production growth yields positive 20-day returns during inflationary periods, contradicting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds three percent while the federal funds rate remains below two percent, deeply negative real rates stimulate aggressive equity buying and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The specified inflation and rate combination produces negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150,000 consistently precedes negative 60-day returns, with a Rank IC below zero across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below one percent annualized triggers recession fears that cause immediate negative equity repricing within one week as investors discount future earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances when quarterly GDP growth drops below the one percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while unemployment falls indicates synchronized economic strengthening that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The industrial production and unemployment divergence yields negative or flat 20-day returns across multiple economic cycles, invalidating the synchronization premise.
